message:
feat(btree,vdbe): thread per-key collation + DESC metadata from VDBE through BtCursor for index compares

Push collation-aware index-key comparison fully down into the BtCursor
layer and expose the metadata the VDBE engine needs to construct
fresh cursors with the right collation/desc state.

## crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs

- New public accessors `index_desc_flags()`, `index_collations()`,
  `collation_registry()` so callers (notably the VDBE engine) can
  read back the metadata that was installed on a cursor and propagate
  it when re-opening or cloning the cursor.
- `cmp_index_values_collated` and `compare_text_with_collation` no
  longer take `&self`. They never read instance state — the registry
  guard was passed in explicitly — and demoting them to associated
  functions removes a borrow that was forcing awkward call-site dances
  in the new VDBE paths. Updated the one in-crate caller accordingly.

## crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs

- New helpers `index_desc_flags_for_cursor` / `index_collations_for_cursor`
  pull metadata for a given cursor id out of the open-cursor table, and
  `storage_cursor_first_index_key_collation` /
  `storage_cursor_first_index_key_equals_probe` resolve the collation
  for the first index key during equality probes. This lets the
  collation-aware autoindex codegen below dispatch with the right
  comparator at runtime.
- Three new tests:
    - `test_open_autoindex_honors_nocase_collation_for_found`
      Equality probe over a NOCASE column hits the autoindex even when
      the literal differs only in case.
    - `test_column_reads_from_key_only_autoindex_without_trailing_rowid`
      Key-only autoindexes (no trailing rowid) still serve column
      reads correctly via the cursor accessors.
    - `test_count_index_eq_run_honors_nocase_collation`
      `COUNT(*) ... WHERE col = 'X'` over a NOCASE column uses the
      collation-aware index range and counts case-insensitively.

## crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs

- New helpers `collation_names_equivalent` (treats `None`/`"BINARY"`
  as the same default for index lookup) and `join_effective_collation`
  (resolves the operative collation for a join column by walking the
  schema + ON-clause hints).
- Five new tests pin the codegen contract for collation-aware
  materialized autoindexes and join paths:
    - `test_codegen_select_large_collated_in_list_materializes_autoindex_with_collation`
    - `test_codegen_select_uncorrelated_collated_in_subquery_materializes_autoindex_with_collation`
    - `test_codegen_select_count_star_indexed_collated_in_list_threads_nocase`
    - `test_extract_count_indexed_in_target_rejects_collation_mismatch`
      Guards the planner from picking an index whose collation does
      not match the comparison's effective collation.
    - `test_codegen_join_on_nocase_column_emits_collation`
